questions.q: An Awareness campaign is evaluated by:
options: Reach, frequency and estimated ad recall lift
questions.explanation: Awareness doesn't optimize for clicks or sales; it's measured by recall and efficient reach.

options: Conversions that would NOT have happened without the ads
The creative with the highest CTR
questions.explanation: It's the causal effect; it's measured with a holdout (control).

questions.q: The client doubts whether the sales would have happened without ads. Methodology:
questions.explanation: Conversion Lift isolates the incremental conversions with a control group.

questions.q: Measure the change in recall and intent of a brand campaign:
questions.explanation: Brand Lift measures brand metrics via survey between exposed and control groups.

questions.q: Compare two creatives to see which performs better with separate audiences:
questions.explanation: A/B Test compares variants with separate audiences to avoid contamination.

questions.q: The CMO wants to see how ALL channels (TV, search, social, offline) contribute to annual sales:
questions.explanation: MMM models the aggregate impact of all channels at a macro level.

options: Changes which conversions are credited to Meta, not the real sales
questions.explanation: The window affects the reported credit, not the real sales.

options: Attribution = credit per touchpoint; incrementality = causal effect of the ads
questions.explanation: Attribution distributes credit; incrementality asks what happened as a result of the ads.

questions.q: Which of these are Meta experiments? Select 3.
questions.explanation: A/B Test, Conversion Lift and Brand Lift are experiments. Bid Cap and Advantage+ placements are not.

questions.q: Measurement plan for a client who demands proof of causal value:
options: Conversion Lift as the core + attribution for daily optimization
questions.explanation: Causality = lift as the core; attribution complements operations but doesn't prove causality on its own.
